detail.limits-hint: Leave empty for unlimited. Limits apply to all transactions (payments + AI usage).

ai.title: AI Gateway
ai.desc: Settings for AI relay and request logging
ai.request-logging-desc: Store full request/response bodies in Redis (7-day TTL). Useful for debugging and auditing AI usage.
ai.default-markup-desc: Markup % for agents that don't set their own. Consumer keys inherit from their agent, which inherits from this global default.

api-keys.title: API Keys
api-keys.desc: Manage API keys for programmatic access to your account
api-keys.table-empty: No API keys yet. Create one to get started.

created.title: API Key Created
created.warn: Copy your secret now — it won't be shown again.

confirm-revoke.title: Revoke API Key
confirm-revoke.desc: This will permanently revoke this API key. Any applications using it will lose access immediately. This action cannot be undone.

confirm-rotate.title: Rotate Secret
confirm-rotate.desc: This will generate a new secret and immediately invalidate the old one. Any applications using the current secret will need to be updated.

confirm-delete.title: Delete API Key
confirm-delete.desc: This will permanently delete this API key. This action cannot be undone.

auth-guide.title: Authentication Methods
auth-guide.desc: Three ways to authenticate API requests — use whichever fits your client best
auth-guide.priority: Priority: X-API-Key > Basic > Bearer. When multiple headers are present, the highest-priority method is used.

admin.login-desc: Connect your wallet and sign in to access the admin panel
admin.not-admin: This account is not registered as an admin. Please contact the system administrator.
admin.footer-note: Admin access is restricted to registered admin wallets only.

gateway.title: Gateway Resilience
gateway.desc: Configure rate limiting, circuit breakers, timeouts, and queue backpressure

rate-limit.title: Rate Limiting
rate-limit.desc: Control request rates by IP, token, or globally

circuit-breaker.title: Circuit Breakers
circuit-breaker.desc: Protect upstream services from cascading failures

timeout.title: Timeouts
timeout.desc: Maximum wait time for external calls
timeout.overrides-desc: Set a longer first-byte wait by provider or gateway model. Model-only entries match every provider.

queue.title: Write Queue
queue.desc: Backpressure limits for async DB write queues

key-providers.title: Key Providers

detail.chart-title: Cost vs revenue share by key

dialog.delete-body: Delete "{{name}}"? All owned keys and revenue records will be permanently removed.

form.related-models-hint: One model per line, comma-separated also works. Use * suffix for prefix matching.

announce.send-confirm-desc: This will broadcast the announcement to all users who subscribed to system announcements. This action cannot be undone.
announce.delete-confirm-desc: Are you sure you want to delete this announcement?
announce.resend-confirm-desc: This will re-broadcast the announcement. Users who previously dismissed it will see it again.

admins.title: Admin Members

login-strategies.title: Login Strategies
login-strategies.desc: Configure which authentication methods are available to users
login-strategies.confirm-clear-desc: Clear the saved credentials (Client ID & Secret)?

siwe.title: Wallet (SIWE)
siwe.desc: Web3 wallet signature login — always enabled as the default authentication method.

credentials.title: Email & Password
credentials.desc: Traditional email and password authentication with argon2 hashing.
credentials.hint: No additional configuration needed. Users can register with email and password on the login page.

google.desc: Allow users to sign in with their Google account.
google.hint: Get credentials at console.cloud.google.com → APIs & Services → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client IDs

github.desc: Allow users to sign in with their GitHub account.
github.hint: Get credentials at github.com → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps

oidc.desc: Enterprise SSO via OIDC — supports Okta, Azure AD, Keycloak, Auth0, etc.
oidc.hint: Enter the OpenID Connect issuer URL from your IdP. Discovery endpoint is auto-derived: {issuer}/.well-known/openid-configuration

saml.title: SAML 2.0 (SSO)
saml.desc: Enterprise SSO via SAML — supports ADFS, Shibboleth, PingFederate, etc.
saml.certificate-ph: Paste IdP signing certificate (PEM or base64)
saml.sp-metadata-hint: Share this URL with your IdP administrator
saml.hint: Enter the IdP metadata URL to auto-discover settings, or configure manually below.

validation.github-id: Expected format: Iv1.xxx, Ov23xxx, or 20-char hex

validation.oidc-issuer: Issuer URL must start with https://
validation.missing-saml: SAML: Entity ID, SSO URL and Certificate are required
validation.saml-sso-url: SSO URL must start with https://

notif.confirm-clear-desc: Clear the saved credentials for this channel? You can keep them if you plan to re-enable later.

validation.whatsapp-phone-id-invalid: Phone Number ID must be a 9-20 digit number

brand.desc: Route requests to any provider through a single OpenAI-compatible API. Load balancing, failover, and usage tracking — all from one dashboard.

hero.ai-badge: Compatible with Claude Code, OpenAI SDK, and Gemini SDK
hero.ai-title-2: One Endpoint for Multiple Model Providers
hero.ai-tagline: Unified Access · Smart Routing · Usage Billing
hero.ai-desc: Tailored for Claude Code, while remaining compatible with the OpenAI SDK, Gemini SDK, and other clients. Includes upstream routing, failover, access control, and usage billing.

hero-chat.q1: Summarize Q4 results in 3 bullet points
hero-chat.a1:
Here are the Q4 highlights:

1. Revenue grew 23% YoY to $4.2M
2. Customer retention improved to 94%
3. Operating margin expanded by 340bps
hero-chat.q2: Write a Python merge sort helper
hero-chat.q3: Translate to Japanese: The meeting is at 3pm
hero-chat.a3:
会議は午後3時です。
(Kaigi wa gogo san-ji desu.)

This is polite, business-appropriate Japanese suitable for email or formal communication.
hero-chat.a4:
Key differences:

- REST returns fixed structures; GraphQL lets clients pick fields
- REST uses multiple URLs; GraphQL uses one endpoint
- REST often over-fetches; GraphQL eliminates this
- REST needs URL versioning; GraphQL evolves via schema
hero-chat.a5:
A distributed system can guarantee only 2 of 3:

- Consistency: reads return the latest write
- Availability: every request gets a response
- Partition tolerance: survives network splits

Since partitions are unavoidable, you choose CP or AP.

cta.ai-title: Bring Your AI Traffic to One Endpoint
cta.ai-desc: Connect providers, configure models and access policies, then serve traffic through one stable endpoint.

intro.label: AI Gateway Platform
intro.title: One Endpoint for Leading Models
intro.desc: Tailored for Claude Code, while also connecting OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and other leading model providers through one gateway endpoint.

unified.title: Unified Access
unified.desc: Point Claude Code, the OpenAI SDK, the Gemini SDK, and other compatible clients at the same gateway endpoint. One entry point means less provider-specific integration work.

keypool.title: Smart Routing & Failover
keypool.desc: Route traffic across providers, upstreams, and API keys with weighted selection. Automatically fail over on errors, rate limits, or exhausted quotas.

metering.title: Usage, Budgets & Billing
metering.desc: Track tokens and cost per request with breakdowns by model, provider, and consumer key. Support balance deduction, budget controls, and history.

features.title: Production Features for an AI Gateway
features.desc: More than request forwarding: governance, observability, and operations built in

relay.title: Unified Multi-Provider Access
relay.desc: Tailored for Claude Code, while connecting OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and multiple AI providers through one endpoint. A single entry point reduces integration and migration overhead.

rotation.title: Upstream Routing & Resilience
rotation.desc: Run multiple upstreams with weighted routing, automatic failover, circuit breakers, timeout controls, and queue backpressure.

consumer.title: Consumer Key Governance
consumer.desc: Issue separate keys for users or applications with model allowlists, rate limits, budget caps, and suspension policies.

billing.title: Usage Metering & Billing
billing.desc: Track requests, tokens, and spend by model, provider, and consumer key, with real-time balance deduction.

logging.title: Logs, Alerts & Notifications
logging.desc: Review request logs, error trends, and upstream health, then notify teams through email, Telegram, webhooks, or WhatsApp.

wallet.title: Crypto-Friendly
wallet.desc: Support automatic top-ups on Base and Polygon, plus 402 payment protocol integration for smoother funding and payment flows.

how.desc: Skip per-provider integrations. After setup, one endpoint handles your AI traffic.
how.title: Go Live in Minutes

step1.title: Connect Providers
step1.desc: Add provider credentials, configure upstreams, and sync or manage your model catalog.

step2.title: Define Access Policies
step2.desc: Create consumer keys and define model access, budgets, rate limits, and billing rules.

step3.title: Point to One Endpoint
step3.desc: Point Claude Code, the OpenAI SDK, the Gemini SDK, or any compatible client at your gateway endpoint.

step4.title: Monitor & Operate
step4.desc: Monitor production traffic with logs, usage reports, upstream health, webhooks, and notification channels.

hub.title: Prismix Documentation
hub.desc: Guides, deployment options, and security architecture for your AI gateway.
hub.security-desc: Multi-layered defense-in-depth design protecting the gateway, credentials, and upstream APIs.
hub.architecture-desc: Strategy pattern, request flow, signing engines, gateway hot path, and project structure.

production.title: Production Deployment
production.desc: Full production stack with persistent database, distributed cache, and multi-instance scaling support.

arch.title: Architecture
arch.prismix: Node.js app serving both the API and the React SPA
arch.caddy: Reverse proxy with automatic Let's Encrypt HTTPS certificates
arch.postgres: Production database with full ACID compliance
arch.redis: WriteThroughCache store + cross-instance event invalidation via Pub/Sub

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.linux: A Linux server (Ubuntu 22.04+, Debian 12+, or similar)
requirements.docker: Docker Engine 24+ and Docker Compose v2
requirements.domain: A domain name pointing to your server (for HTTPS)

external-db.title: External PostgreSQL / Redis
external-db.desc: To use managed database or Redis: remove the postgres/redis services from docker-compose.yml, set DATABASE_URL and/or REDIS_URL in .env.local, and remove corresponding depends_on entries.

behind-proxy.title: Behind existing reverse proxy (Nginx/Traefik)
behind-proxy.desc: Remove the caddy service from docker-compose.yml and point your existing proxy to localhost:3403.

eacces.fix: Stop existing web server, or use custom port

https.fix: Check dig your-domain.com resolves to your IP

pg-refused.fix: Wait for health check; check docker compose logs postgres

security.title: Security Architecture
security.desc: Multi-layered security design protecting the AI gateway, upstream APIs, and user credentials.

arch.title: Defense-in-Depth Architecture
arch.desc: Every request passes through multiple security layers before reaching the upstream API. Each layer is independent — a bypass of one layer does not compromise the others.

authn.title: Authentication
authn.login-desc: SIWE (Sign-In with Ethereum) — users prove wallet ownership by signing a server-issued nonce.
authn.nonce-desc: Cryptographic nonces (128-bit, single-use, 5-minute TTL) with scope isolation between user and admin flows.
authn.access-desc: JWT (HS256) with 15-minute expiry, enforced issuer/audience claims, and role-based access control.
authn.refresh-desc: 256-bit opaque tokens stored as SHA-256 hashes in the database — a DB breach does not expose usable tokens.
authn.scope-desc: User and admin authentication use separate middleware, nonce scopes, and token roles. Cross-role escalation is structurally prevented.

encryption.title: Credential Encryption
encryption.aes: All signing engine credentials are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with authenticated encryption.
encryption.pbkdf2: Encryption keys are derived via PBKDF2-SHA256 (100,000 iterations) with per-domain key derivation.
encryption.mask: API responses return masked credential values (e.g., sk-a***xyz) — raw secrets are never exposed to the frontend.
encryption.log: Gateway logs mask sensitive headers (Authorization, X-API-Key, X-Signature) before writing to the database.

sandbox.title: Custom Script Sandbox
sandbox.desc: User-provided signing scripts run in a QuickJS WebAssembly sandbox — completely isolated from the host system.
sandbox.wasm-desc: Scripts execute in WebAssembly memory space with no access to host filesystem, network, or process.
sandbox.memory-desc: 8 MB per runtime — prevents memory exhaustion attacks.
sandbox.cpu-desc: Configurable timeout (default 100ms, max 5s) enforced via interrupt handler — stops infinite loops.
sandbox.context-desc: Fresh execution context per invocation — no state leaks between script runs.
sandbox.size-desc: 64 KB maximum script size — prevents storage abuse.
sandbox.output-desc: Script output headers are validated against RFC 7230 — forbidden transport-level headers are blocked.
sandbox.error-desc: Script error messages are truncated to 200 characters — prevents information leakage.

network.title: Network Security
network.rate-limit: Multi-dimensional rate limiting: per-IP, per-token, per-resource, and global — all configurable via admin dashboard.
network.circuit-breaker: Circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures when upstream services are unhealthy.
network.timeout: Configurable upstream request timeouts with AbortController-based cancellation.
network.hop-by-hop: Hop-by-hop header stripping per RFC 7230 §6.1 — prevents HTTP request smuggling.
network.cors: Per-resource CORS policies with preflight handling before payment verification — browser-side access control.
network.proxy-headers: Standard proxy forwarding headers (X-Forwarded-For/Proto/Host, X-Request-ID) for traceability.

validation.title: Input Validation
validation.headers: Custom headers validated against RFC 7230 — name format, value encoding, control character rejection.
validation.cors: CORS configuration validated at save time — origin URL format, HTTP method whitelist, max-age bounds.
validation.auth-profile: Signing engine configurations validated by each engine's validateConfig before database write.
validation.forbidden: 11 HTTP transport-level headers (Host, Content-Length, Transfer-Encoding, Connection, etc.) are blocked from user configuration.

errors.title: Error Handling
errors.prod: Production error responses return generic messages — no stack traces, internal paths, or system details exposed.
errors.script: Custom script errors are truncated and sanitized before returning to the caller.
errors.upstream: Upstream connection failures return standardized error responses without leaking internal network topology.

arch.title: System Architecture
arch.desc: Gateway pipeline, authentication, multi-tenancy, signing engines, and performance.

flow.title: Gateway Pipeline
flow.desc: One HTTP round-trip: 402 → pay → verified → forwarded → done. The hot path never blocks on database I/O. All writes are async.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.desc: Pluggable auth strategy system supporting 4 login methods: SIWE (wallet-based), credentials (email/password), Google OAuth, and GitHub OAuth. JWT access tokens (15min) + single-use refresh tokens (7d). User and admin auth are fully isolated — separate scopes, separate middleware, separate context. The identities table maps auth providers to user accounts.

tenant.title: Multi-Tenancy
tenant.desc: All data entities are system-level. Resources, auth profiles, agents, and settings are globally managed by the admin. SSE events use scope-based filtering for admin and user isolation.

db.title: Database & Upgrades
db.desc: Schema management, migration workflow, and upgrade procedures.

supported.title: Supported Databases
supported.switch-note: PostgreSQL is the only supported database engine. Set DATABASE_URL to connect.

migration.title: Migration System
migration.intro: Migrations are generated by drizzle-kit from the TypeScript schema. Seed data lives separately in deploy/seed/. The schema files in src/server/db/schemas/ are the single source of truth.

startup.title: Startup Behaviour

upgrade.behaviour: User runs pnpm db:migrate before restarting

startup.safety-note: Existing databases are never modified automatically. This protects production data — operators always have a chance to back up before applying schema changes.

migrate.what: Execute pending migrations against the database

reset.what: Wipe DB + regenerate migrations from scratch

push.what: Push schema directly to DB (no migration file)

no-delete.rule: Never delete migration files after release
no-delete.reason: Breaks hash tracking in user databases — migrate() will fail

no-modify.reason: drizzle-orm skips already-applied hashes — schema will drift silently

sync-schemas.reason: Both dialects must define identical tables and columns

backup-first.rule: Always back up before running db:migrate
backup-first.reason: Schema changes may be irreversible — a backup is your safety net

generate-both.rule: Always run db:generate for both dialects

pre-release.title: Pre-release (before v1.0)
pre-release.desc: Migrations are disposable. Reset freely. db:reset deletes database files, clears generated migration SQL, and runs drizzle-kit generate to produce a single clean init migration.

post-release.title: Post-release (v1.0+)
post-release.desc: Existing migration files are immutable. Never delete or modify them — users' databases depend on them. Generate incremental migrations with db:generate, test locally, then ship.

seed.title: Seed Data
seed.desc: Default networks and tokens are defined in deploy/seed/<dialect>.sql. These files are only executed on first deploy — not on restarts or upgrades.
seed.existing: To add new default data for existing deployments, include INSERT statements in the incremental migration SQL (generated by db:generate), or document manual INSERT commands in the release notes.

migration-fail.fix: Check migration SQL for syntax errors. Run pnpm db:reset to regenerate.

schema-drift.symptom: Schema drift after manual DB edits
schema-drift.fix: Use pnpm db:push to force-sync, or generate a migration that accounts for the current state.

bad-migration.fix: Restore from backup, fix the migration SQL, ship a patch release, then re-run db:migrate.

brand.title: Brand Guidelines
brand.desc: Logo, colors, typography, and design system conventions.

overview.title: Brand Overview
overview.desc: Prismix is a self-hosted AI gateway with unified multi-provider API access. Monochrome design, developer-first, infrastructure-native.

technical.desc: Built for developers, protocol-native, open standards

modern.desc: Monochrome aesthetic, geometric precision, flat design

logo.title: Logo
logo.desc: The Prismix icon is composed of three layered elements: corner brackets (the "Gate" frame), a C arc ring (the "Coin" + gateway opening), and a center dot (the transaction origin).
logo.dark-when: Light mode — high contrast on light backgrounds
logo.light-when: Dark mode — high contrast on dark backgrounds
logo.wordmark-desc: The wordmark is "prismix" in all lowercase. It inherits currentColor for automatic light/dark adaptation.
logo.favicon-desc: Simplified icon for small sizes (16–48px). Only the C ring + center dot — corner brackets are omitted for clarity at small scale.
logo.meaning-coin: The C ring and center dot form an abstract coin.
logo.meaning-gate: The C ring's opening is the passage; the corner brackets are the frame.
logo.meaning-together: Together they read as Prismix: a coin passing through a gate.
logo.lockup-desc: The full logo lockup combines the icon (28px) and wordmark (15px) horizontally, vertically centered, with an 8px gap. Optional subtitle appears below the wordmark in 10px muted text.

colors.title: Color System
colors.desc: Monochrome design system with achromatic tokens. Accent colors (green, blue, red) are reserved for status indicators only — never in the logo or brand materials.

black.name: Black
black.usage: Dark icon background, primary surfaces (dark mode)

colors.accent-note: Accent colors (Emerald #10B981, Blue #3B82F6, Red #EF4444) are functional only — used for status indicators and data visualization, never in logo or brand materials.

usage.title: Logo Usage Rules
usage.approved: Dark icon on light backgrounds, light icon on dark backgrounds. Icon + wordmark horizontal lockup. Icon standalone at favicon size. Wordmark in currentColor.

no-dark-on-dark.rule: Use dark icon on dark backgrounds
no-dark-on-dark.reason: Use the light variant for contrast

no-arc-angle.reason: 60° is part of the brand identity

no-outline.rule: Add outlines or borders around the icon
no-outline.reason: The rounded-rect background is the boundary

no-combine.rule: Combine with other logos without clear space

no-font-change.rule: Change the wordmark font or weight
no-font-change.reason: Plus Jakarta Sans ExtraBold is fixed

components.title: Component Library & Icons
components.desc: UI components follow the shadcn/ui architecture with Radix UI primitives. Icons from Lucide React.

dialogs.title: Dialog Layering
dialogs.desc: Keep dialog interiors flat by default. If the content area already has more than two border layers, stop adding outlined containers and switch to borderless section cards with soft surface contrast.

cap-borders.guideline: More than two border layers makes a dialog feel crowded and heavy, so remove inner outlines first.

use-surface-cards.guideline: When sections need stronger separation, use borderless cards with radius, spacing, and soft background contrast instead of another box outline.

card-style.guideline: Use soft secondary/accent surfaces that make grouping visible without overpowering form controls or body text.

keep-focus.rule: Emphasize only one primary action zone
keep-focus.guideline: Payment info, help text, and user input can be separated, but each dialog should still have a single strongest visual focus.

developer.guideline: Lead with how it works, not why it's great

neutral.guideline: No exclamation marks, no hype language

clear-space.title: Clear Space & Minimum Size
clear-space.desc: Minimum clear space around the logo equals 25% of the icon height on all sides.
clear-space.small-note: Below 24px the corner brackets may be omitted — the C ring + center dot alone is sufficient for recognition.

surfaces.title: Backgrounds & Surfaces
surfaces.desc: Dark mode is the default for marketing materials. Light mode for dashboards and documentation.

seo.title: SEO & Social
seo.desc: Meta tags, structured data, and social sharing configuration.

title-tag.content: Prismix — Self-Hosted AI Gateway for Unified API Access

seo.structured-desc: JSON-LD for SoftwareApplication, Organization, and FAQPage — enables rich snippets in search results.
seo.files-desc: robots.txt (allow homepage, block private routes), sitemap.xml (XML with hreflang), site.webmanifest (PWA manifest).

403.title: Access Denied
403.desc: You don't have permission to view this page.

404.title: Page Not Found
404.desc: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

ledger.title: Transaction Ledger
ledger.desc: All wallet transactions — deposits, withdrawals, AI charges, and refunds

fiat.title: Payment Methods
fiat.desc: Configure fiat payment methods for agent top-ups

valid.unsupported-image-type: Only PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP images are accepted

form.qr-code-upload-hint: Upload a QR image and it will be stored directly in the system.

fiat.delete-confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this payment method? This action cannot be undone.

topup.title: Top-up Orders
topup.desc: View and manage created deposit orders

detail.pending-hint: For pending orders, credit the wallet and confirm the order, or reject it if the request is invalid.
detail.readonly-hint: Processed orders are read-only and kept for audit and reconciliation.

action.settle-fiat-hint: Record the user's actual fiat payment amount ({{currency}}).
action.settle-credit-hint: This amount will be added to the user's wallet balance in USDC.

reject-reason.wrong_network: Deposit was sent on the wrong network

form.name: Name
form.upstream-routing-strategy-hint: Controls how multiple upstreams under this provider are selected by default.
form.official-concurrency-limit-desc: Maximum in-flight requests sent to the provider official base URL at the same time.
form.official-queue-timeout-desc: How long a request may wait for an official upstream slot when the limit is reached.
form.sigv4-region-hint: The AWS region for your Bedrock endpoint
form.sigv4-access-key-id-hint: Your IAM access key ID for Bedrock API access
form.cloudflare-client-id-hint: The service-token client ID sent as CF-Access-Client-Id

dialog.delete-body: Delete "{{name}}"? All models and keys will also be deleted.
dialog.delete-upstream-body: Remove upstream "{{name}}" from this provider? All keys bound to this upstream will also be deleted.

upstreams.desc: Configure additional execution endpoints for one provider and control their routing order or weights.
upstreams.official-default-desc: Currently uses the provider base URL as the default execution endpoint.
upstreams.official-fallback-desc: Falls back to the provider base URL when higher-priority upstreams are unavailable.

form.no-available: All upstreams are already assigned to this provider.

keys.desc: API keys grouped by upstream. Keys in each pool are load-balanced according to the provider strategy.
keys.empty: No keys in this pool yet
keys.official-hint: Fallback pool — uses the provider base URL (priority 1000)
keys.weight-hint: Weight — higher value means more traffic share

form.name: Key Name
form.api-key-hint: Your key will be encrypted and stored securely
form.cloudflare-client-secret-ph: Enter your Cloudflare Access client secret
form.cloudflare-client-secret-hint: Stored encrypted and sent as CF-Access-Client-Secret
form.secret-access-key-ph: Enter your AWS secret access key
form.secret-access-key-hint: Your AWS secret access key will be encrypted and stored securely
form.owner-hint: Assign this key to a third-party provider for revenue sharing

dialog.delete-body: Delete key "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

form.name: Name

form.limited-free-hint: Model is automatically disabled when the expiry passes. Re-enable manually after setting prices.

form.gray-users-search-ph: Search users by name or email

discover.no-key: No API key configured for this provider. Add a key first to discover models.

dialog.batch-delete-body: Delete {{count}} selected models? This cannot be undone.
dialog.clean-zero-body: Delete {{count}} models with $0 pricing that are not currently limited-free? Models without valid pricing cannot be billed and may be abused.

sync.title: Sync Prices
sync.desc: Compare current prices with LiteLLM catalog. Select models to update.
sync.no-diff: All prices are up to date with LiteLLM catalog.

routes.title: Provider Routes
routes.desc: Configure which providers serve this model. Lower priority = tried first.
routes.empty: No routes configured. Add a provider route to enable this model.

form.provider-model-id-hint: Leave empty to use the model's own ID

dialog.delete-body: Remove the route to {{provider}}? The model will no longer be served via this provider.

user-models.title: Model Catalog
user-models.desc: Available models and pricing for your account

ai-relay.title: AI Endpoints
ai-relay.desc: Separate OpenAI and Anthropic base URLs with shared routing, billing, and usage tracking

intro.title: What are AI Endpoints?
intro.body: Prismix exposes separate OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible base URLs. Each endpoint has its own model catalog while sharing provider routing, billing, and usage tracking behind the scenes.
intro.benefits: Protocol-specific URLs | Per-key billing & rate limits | Native SDK compatibility

endpoint.title: Base URLs
endpoint.hint-before: Use this base URL with your
endpoint.hint-after: . Use the OpenAI URL for OpenAI SDKs and the Anthropic URL for Claude Code; Anthropic clients append /v1 automatically.

recent.title: Recent Requests
recent.empty: No usage data yet. Send a request through the relay to see stats.

request-log.not-found: Request log not available — logging may be disabled or the record has expired.

ai-logs.title: AI Gateway Logs
ai-logs.desc: Request-level logs for AI model relay

detail.title: Request #{{id}}
detail.no-body: Request logging is not enabled or log has expired

ai-errors.title: AI Gateway Errors
ai-errors.desc: 4xx / 5xx error overview and recent error trends

ai-upstreams.title: AI Upstreams
ai-upstreams.desc: Inspect configured upstreams, recent traffic, and error conditions
ai-upstreams.empty: No upstreams have been configured yet

stats.active-subtitle: Received traffic in the last 24 hours
stats.degraded-subtitle: Elevated error rate in the last 30 minutes

dialog.delete-body: This upstream and all its provider assignments will be removed. Keys bound to it will fall back to the default provider URL.

form.name: Display Name

form.concurrency-limit-desc: Maximum in-flight requests sent to this upstream at the same time.
form.queue-timeout-desc: How long a request may wait for an upstream slot before trying another upstream.

detail.health-trend-desc: Hourly request volume, errors, and latency over the last 24 hours

wallet.connect-hint: Connect a wallet if you want to use onchain deposit and withdrawal flows.

keys.title: My API Keys
keys.empty: No API keys yet. Create one to get started.

settings.title: Settings
settings.wallet-desc: View your on-chain wallet address and the blockchain networks currently supported.
settings.wallet-address-empty: No wallet address is available yet.
settings.wallet-networks-empty: No blockchain networks are available right now.

endpoint.title: API Endpoint
endpoint.desc: Connect Claude Code and OpenAI SDK clients through separate protocol-specific endpoints
endpoint.intro-body: If Claude Code is your main client, point ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at the Anthropic endpoint. OpenAI SDK clients should use the OpenAI endpoint. Prismix handles model routing, usage tracking, and billing behind the scenes.
endpoint.url-hint: Use the OpenAI URL for OpenAI SDKs and the Anthropic URL as ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL for Claude Code; Anthropic clients append /v1 automatically.
endpoint.no-keys: You don't have any API keys yet. Create one to get started.

wallet.title: Wallet
wallet.desc: Manage your USDC balance, deposits, and withdrawals
wallet.balance-tooltip: This site settles in USDC. Whether you deposit with crypto or fiat, the final credited balance is converted and recorded in USDC.
wallet.selector-deposit-crypto-desc: Use the existing on-chain USDC deposit flow: create an order, transfer funds, then verify.
wallet.selector-deposit-fiat-desc: Choose an admin-configured payment method, pay offline, and submit a manual review order.
wallet.selector-withdraw-crypto-desc: Submit an on-chain USDC withdrawal request for admin review and transfer.
wallet.selector-withdraw-fiat-desc: Submit a fiat withdrawal order for manual payout and review.
wallet.deposit-step-create-desc: Choose the network and amount first. After the order is created, the dialog will switch to deposit verification.
wallet.deposit-step-verify-desc: Send USDC to the generated address on the selected network, then paste the transaction hash for immediate credit.
wallet.deposit-qr-card-desc: Use a wallet app to scan this code and fill the deposit address instantly.
wallet.deposit-manual-desc: Already sent? Paste your transaction hash for instant verification
wallet.withdraw-desc: Send USDC from your wallet to an external address
wallet.fiat-deposit-desc: Choose an admin-configured payment method, pay offline, and submit the order for manual review.
wallet.fiat-deposit-success: Fiat deposit order submitted, pending admin review
wallet.fiat-deposit-failed: Failed to create fiat deposit order
wallet.fiat-proof-desc: Complete the payment using the order details and provide payment proof for admin review.
wallet.fiat-proof-ph: Upload your payment screenshot or transfer proof
wallet.fiat-proof-upload-hint: The uploaded proof image will be stored directly in the system.
wallet.fiat-proof-hint: The admin will verify your payment based on the proof image you upload.
wallet.fiat-proof-unsupported-type: Only PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP images are accepted
wallet.fiat-withdraw-desc: Enter your payout method, payout details, and amount. The admin will review and process it manually.
wallet.fiat-withdraw-method-ph: e.g. Alipay, bank transfer, WeChat, FPS
wallet.fiat-withdraw-info-ph: e.g. account number, name, bank, and other required details
wallet.fiat-withdraw-note-ph: e.g. preferred account or extra payout instructions
wallet.fiat-withdraw-success: Fiat withdrawal order submitted, pending admin review
wallet.fiat-withdraw-failed: Failed to create fiat withdrawal order
wallet.fiat-withdraw-hint: Make sure your payout details are accurate. After approval, the admin will send funds offline using the method you provided.
wallet.fiat-methods-empty: No fiat methods are available right now.
wallet.fiat-config-empty: No extra instructions are available for this method.
wallet.deposit-min-amount-hint: Minimum deposit: {{amount}} USDC, up to {{decimals}} decimals
wallet.deposit-order-created: Deposit order created. Auto-detection is active for the next hour.
wallet.deposit-pending-block-title: You already have a pending deposit order
wallet.deposit-pending-block-desc: Complete pending order #{{orderId}} from the deposit orders list before creating a new one.
wallet.deposit-order-active: Waiting for {{amount}} USDC. Auto-detection runs in the background for up to 1 hour.
wallet.deposit-order-ended: This order can no longer be verified. Create a new order to continue depositing.
wallet.deposit-result-summary: {{amount}} USDC has been credited to your wallet.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.desc: View wallet deposits, withdrawals, payments, and AI usage history
